name: Chiapas (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the lush vegetation of the state
the rivers and skies of Chiapas
the soil and mountains of the Sierra Madre de Chiapas

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Chiapas centred.
history: Chiapas has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms feature the Sumidero Canyon, two lions guarding a castle on a green hill flanked by palms, and a crown above — derived from the arms granted to the city of Ciudad Real (now San Cristobal de las Casas) by Charles V in 1535.
